questions.sentence: The Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP) is the final piece of the SOLID acronym and is crucial for building decoupled and testable frontend architectures.
DIP states two main things: high-level modules should not depend on low-level modules—both should depend on abstractions—and abstractions should not depend on details.
In a React or Vue application, this often means your UI components should not directly instantiate API services or data providers.
Instead, you should inject these dependencies (Dependency Injection) through props or specialized providers.
Adhering to DIP makes your codebase more flexible and allows for a clearer separation of concerns between your business logic and your presentation layer.
